caption: The video appears to be an educational tutorial on cryogenic engineering, specifically focusing on the concept of liquid yield. The slide titled "Tutorial - 2" presents a detailed explanation involving a table with various thermodynamic properties such as pressure (p), temperature (T), enthalpy (h), and entropy (s) for different states labeled as 1, 2, and f. A formula for liquid yield is shown: \( y = \frac{(h_1 - h_2)}{(h_1 - h_f)} \). The video progresses by substituting values from the table into this formula, calculating the liquid yield as \( \frac{(462 - 430)}{(462 - 29)} = \frac{32}{433} \). Accompanying the numerical data is a phase diagram illustrating constant temperature (T=const) and constant pressure (p=const) lines, along with a path representing changes in enthalpy (h=const). This visual aid helps in understanding the thermodynamic processes involved in cryogenic systems. The presentation is given by Prof. M D Atrey from the Department of Mechanical Engineering at IIT Bombay.
